[(195 sum—~11 mu] -- 3 of 6 -- THE GAZErrE OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY [Pm 11mm. 300] MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS NOTIFICATION 1 New Delhi, the 12th March, 20l3 50- 626(19— Whereas by notification of the Government of India in the erstwhile Ministry . of Surface Transport (Road Wings) number 8.0. 78 (E), dated 04/ 02 / 1999’ and SO. 1050 (E), dated 24/04/2009 issued under Section 11 of the National Highways Authority of India Act, 1988 (68 of 1988), the Central Government has entrusted the stretch from kilometer 366.200 to kilometer 396.000 (Kishangarh —- Ajmer section), from kilometer 0.000 to kilometer 388.000 (Ajmer — Rajasthan/ Gujarat Border section) of National Highway No. 8 and km 364.000 to km 366.200 (Kishangarh to Bewar section)of National Highway No. 8 in the State of Rajasthan respectively to the National Highways Authority of India (hereinafter referred to as the “Authority”); And whereas, pursuant to the provision of Section 14 of the National Highways Authority of India Act, 1988 (68 of 1988), the Authority has entered into an Agreement (hereinafter referred to as said agreement) with M /s Soma Isolux KisI .igarh Beawar Tollway Private Linhitcd having its registered office at 8‘11 Floor, Block B, Vatika Towers, Golf Links Road, Se¢tor-54, Gurgaon, Haryana, 122002 (hereinafter referred to as the “Concessionaire”), the su¢cessfu1 bidder for bids submitted on 23/ 12/2008 for the development of Kishangarh ~ Ajmer —- Beawar section from Km 364.125 to Km 58.245 on National Highway No. 8 (hereinafter referred to as the “said section”) to six lane operation and maintenance of the said section. Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 8A of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956), read with rule 3 of the National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules, 2008 published vide G.S.R 838(E) dated 5‘11 December, 2008 ( heneinafter referred to as the “Rules”), the Central Government, having regard to the expenditure involved in building, maintenance, management and operation of the said section, interest on the capital invested, reasonable return, the volume of traffic and the period of the agreement entered into between the authority and the concessionaire, hereby nolifies that there shall be levied and collected fees on mechanical vehicles for the use of the1 said section at the rate specified in columns (2) of the Table 1 for net road section length 93.560 km for use of the four and more lane on the Type of Vehicles specified in Table 1, and authorizes the said Concessionaire to collect and retain the fee on and from , the' date of commercial operation or publication of this notification in the Official Gazette, whichever is later, till the termination date as specified in the agreement; -- 4 of 6 -- [am 11—130: 3(ii)] mmmzmm TABLE-l Type of vehicle Base rate of fee per km ' for the base year 2007- 08 (in Rupees) (1) (2) Car, Jeep, Van or Light Motor Vehicle 0.65 Light Commercial Vehicle, Light Goods Vehicle or Mini Bus 1.05 Bus or Truck 2.20 Heavy Construction Machinery (HCM) or Earth Moving 3.45 Equipment (EME) or Multi Axle Vehicle (MAV) (three to six axles) Oversized Vehicles (seven or more axles) 4.20 Notes : - l. The fee levied and collected hereunder shall be due and Plaza for the distance specified for such Toll Plaza(s); payable at the following Toll. Location of Toll Plaza(s) (chainage) Length (in km) for which Fee is payable Km/Ch. 378.800 (near Gegal village in Ajmer district) 46 .780 Km/ Ch. 44.800 (near Pipalaz village in Ajmer district) 46.780 The rate of monthly pass for a person who owns a mechanical vehicle registered for non-commercial purposes and resides within a distance of twenty kilometers from the toll plaza is Rs. 200.00 (Rupees No Hundred OnlyH‘er they»: flan-BM is subject to revision every year as per the provisions of the National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules, 2008. For multiple journey on the highway section, passes shallhe Mwatthefdmg rates, namely. Maximum number of one way journeys allowed Amount Payable Period of validity One and half times of the fee for Two one way journey Twenty four hours, from the time of payment Two-third of amount of the fee Fifty or more payable for fifty or more single journeys. One month from date ' of payment Mechanical vehicle which is loaded in excess of permissible load specified for its category under the Rules, shall be liable to pay fee at such rates which is applicable for the next higher category of the vehicles and shall not be entitled to make use of the highway section unleSs the excess load has been removed from such mechanical vehicle. All definitions including category of mechanical vehicles, its permissible load, category of exempted vehicles, service road, alternative road etc. shall be as per definitions specified in the Rules. . geuz 61/]3'2 -- 5 of 6 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART llvaEC. 3(ii)] The fee rates shall be displayed for information of the users in accordance with rules 12 of the said Rules. . Based on the Base rate of fee per km for the base year 2007—08 as mentionedvin 1 Table—l, the actual amount of fee to be charged from the mechanical vehicles and ‘ the discounts will be calculated by the Authority and revised annually in accordance with the rule 5 of the said rules provided that no revision shall be feffected within six months and the same shall be published in at least one newspaper, each in English and vernacular language accordingly. ‘ [F. No. NHAl/l3013/453/12-13/CMD-CO/GC Kishangarh-Ajmer—Beawar BOT] ALKESH SHARMA, Jt. Secy. Printed by the Manager. Government of India Press, Ring Road.
